entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: The goal of ensuring access to sufficient and nutritious food in challenging environments.

entities.name: NUTRIENT-STRESSED ENVIRONMENTS
entities.description: Environments characterized by low availability of essential nutrients for plant growth.

entities.name: UPLAND CROPS
entities.description: Crops grown in higher altitude or non-irrigated areas, typically in the semi-arid tropics.

entities.name: NUTRIENT UPTAKE
entities.description: The ability of plants to access and absorb nutrients from the soil.

entities.name: NUTRIENT USE EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The ability of plants to use absorbed nutrients efficiently for growth and yield.

entities.name: GENETIC MANIPULATION
entities.description: The process of altering genetic material to enhance specific plant capabilities.

entities.name: GENETIC ENHANCEMENT
entities.description: The use of genetic tools to improve plant traits for better performance.

entities.name: BREEDING
entities.description: The science of developing new plant varieties with desired characteristics.

entities.name: NATURAL RESOURCE MANAGEMENT
entities.description: The sustainable management of environmental resources to support agriculture.

entities.name: SEMI-ARID TROPICS
entities.description: Regions characterized by limited rainfall and high temperatures.

entities.name: SUSTAINABLE CULTIVATION
entities.description: Agricultural practices that maintain productivity and environmental health over time.

entities.name: JAPAN
entities.description: The country that funded the special project on sustainable cultivation of upland crops.
